model.label: Grok 4.2
model.description: xAI • Large reasoning model designed for real-time information access, coding, and general problem solving.

pick.rationale: UConn enters as the No. 2 seed with presumed elite metrics typical of a top-10 team, creating a massive quality gap over Furman ranked around 191-195 in both KenPom and Torvik with middling efficiency from the Southern Conference. The Huskies' depth and experience from a power conference schedule overwhelm a 15-seed that lacks any historical precedent for success in this matchup, as 2-vs-15 upsets have occurred only once in NCAA history. Minor injury concerns with Jaylin Stewart being uncertain do not alter the outcome given the talent disparity and lack of a credible path for Furman to keep it competitive.

reasoningStep.title: Ratings, Injury, and Historical Context Review
reasoningStep.summary: Confirmed Furman's low-mid 190s rankings against UConn's expected top-tier status as a 2-seed; noted minor UConn injuries but no impact on massive mismatch.
evidence: lookup_cbb_ratings showed Furman at 191 KP/195 Torvik with 22-12 record
web searches confirmed UConn injury updates on Stewart (uncertain) but Demary ok
historical 2/15 data and preview articles show no upset path

result.title: Introducing the kPOY | The kenpom.com blog
result.snippet: Kemba Walker, UConn . (.444) Walker has the stats to match up with anybody, but right now the wide gulf between the kenpom rating and the perception of the mainstream media is keeping him this low.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r64-east-8
Current game label: East 2/15
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: UConn (uconn) vs Furman (furman)
Prior committed picks: 11

result.title: Introducing the kPOY | The kenpom.com blog
result.snippet: Kemba Walker, UConn . (.444) Walker has the stats to match up with anybody, but right now the wide gulf between the kenpom rating and the perception of the mainstream media is keeping him this low.
